web_text: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s chosen ones, and the knowledge of the truth which is according to godliness, in hope of eternal life, which God, who can’t lie, promised before time began; but in his own time revealed his word in the message with which I was entrusted according to the commandment of God our Savior; to Titus, my true child according to a common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.
slang_text: It's Paul here, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, matchin' up with the faith of God's chosen crew, and knowin' the truth that leads to godliness, hopin' for eternal life — which God, who can't lie, promised before time even got started; but right on His own schedule, He revealed His word through the message I got entrusted with, on orders from God our Savior; writin' to Titus, my true kid in our shared faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.

web_text: I left you in Crete for this reason, that you would set in order the things that were lacking, and appoint elders in every city, as I directed you; if anyone is blameless, the husband of one wife, having children who believe, who are not accused of loose or unruly behavior. For the overseer must be blameless, as God’s steward; not self-pleasing, not easily angered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for dishonest gain; but given to hospitality, a lover of good, sober minded, fair, holy, self-controlled;
slang_text: I left you back in Crete for exactly this reason: to get everything squared away that was still missin', and appoint elders in every town, same as I already told you — pickin' out guys who are blameless, one wife's husband, with believin' kids who ain't caught up in wild or unruly behavior. 'Cause the overseer's gotta be blameless, actin' as God's steward — not out for himself, not quick-tempered, not into wine, not violent, not chasin' dirty money; but hospitable, into good stuff, level-headed, fair, holy, self-controlled;

web_text: holding to the faithful word which is according to the teaching, that he may be able to exhort in the sound doctrine, and to convict those who contradict him. For there are also many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision, whose mouths must be stopped; men who overthrow whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for dishonest gain’s sake. One of them, a prophet of their own, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, and idle gluttons.”
slang_text: holdin' tight to the trustworthy word that lines up with sound teachin', so he can encourage folks with sound doctrine and call out whoever's arguin' against it. 'Cause there's a ton of unruly guys out there, empty talkers and con artists, especially the circumcision crowd — their mouths gotta get shut down. These guys wreck whole households, teachin' stuff they got no business teachin', all for a dirty buck. One of their own prophets even said, "Cretans are always liars, nasty animals, and lazy gluttons."

web_text: This testimony is true. For this cause, reprove them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith, not paying attention to Jewish fables and commandments of men who turn away from the truth. To the pure, all things are pure; but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled. They profess that they know God, but by their deeds they deny him, being abominable, disobedient, and unfit for any good work.
slang_text: That testimony's true. So call 'em out hard, so they can get sound in the faith again, not gettin' distracted by Jewish fables and rules from folks who turned away from the truth. To the pure, everything's pure; but to the defiled, unbelievin' crowd, nothin's pure — their mind and conscience are both grody through and through. They claim they know God, but their own deeds prove they're denyin' Him, actin' disgustin', disobedient, useless for any good work whatsoever.

web_text: But say the things which fit sound doctrine, that older men should be temperate, sensible, sober minded, sound in faith, in love, and in perseverance: and that older women likewise be reverent in behavior, not slanderers nor enslaved to much wine, teachers of that which is good; that they may train the young wives to love their husbands, to love their children,
slang_text: But you teach whatever fits sound doctrine — that older guys oughta be level-headed, sharp, sober-minded, sound in faith, love, and patience; and that older women, same deal, oughta be respectable, no trash-talkin', not hooked on wine, teachin' whatever's good; so they can train the younger wives to love their husbands, love their kids,

web_text: to be sober minded, chaste, workers at home, kind, being in subjection to their own husbands, that God’s word may not be blasphemed. Likewise, exhort the younger men to be sober minded; in all things showing yourself an example of good works; in your teaching showing integrity, seriousness, incorruptibility, and soundness of speech that can’t be condemned; that he who opposes you may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say about us.
slang_text: stay sober-minded, pure, hard workers at home, kind, yieldin' to their own husbands, so God's word doesn't get blasphemed. Same deal, push the younger guys to be sober-minded too; in every way, be an example of good works yourself; in your teachin', show integrity, seriousness, honesty, and sound talk that can't get shot down — so whoever fights against you ends up ashamed, with nothin' bad to say about us.

web_text: Exhort servants to be in subjection to their own masters, and to be well-pleasing in all things; not contradicting; not stealing, but showing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God, our Savior, in all things. For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men, instructing us to the intent that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we would live soberly, righteously, and godly in this present age;
slang_text: Push servants to yield to their own masters, pleasin' 'em in everything, not talkin' back, not stealin', but showin' total trustworthiness, so they'd make the doctrine of God our Savior look good in every way. 'Cause the grace of God's shown up, bringin' salvation to everybody, teachin' us to ditch ungodliness and worldly cravings, and live level-headed, righteous, and godly lives right now, in this present age;

web_text: looking for the blessed hope and appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ; who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify for himself a people for his own possession, zealous for good works. Say these things and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no man despise you.
slang_text: watchin' out for the blessed hope, the glorious appearin' of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ, who handed Himself over for us, so He could buy us back outta every bit of sin, and purify a people all His own, fired up for good works. Teach this stuff, encourage folks, call stuff out, with total authority. Don't let nobody dis you.

web_text: Remind them to be in subjection to rulers and to authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work, to speak evil of no one, not to be contentious, to be gentle, showing all humility toward all men. For we were also once foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving various lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another. But when the kindness of God our Savior and his love toward mankind appeared,
slang_text: Remind everybody to yield to rulers and authorities, stay obedient, be ready for every good work, talk trash about nobody, don't pick fights, stay gentle, showin' humility to everybody. 'Cause we used to be foolish too, disobedient, conned, servin' every kinda lust and pleasure, livin' in malice and envy, hateful, hatin' each other. But then the kindness of God our Savior and His love for humanity showed up,

web_text: not by works of righteousness which we did ourselves, but according to his mercy, he saved us through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit, whom he poured out on us richly, through Jesus Christ our Savior; that being justified by his grace, we might be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life. This saying is faithful, and concerning these things I desire that you affirm confidently, so that those who have believed God may be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable to men;
slang_text: not 'cause of righteous stuff we did ourselves, but 'cause of His mercy — He saved us through the washin' of bein' born new and renewed by the Holy Spirit, who He poured out on us rich and generous, through Jesus Christ our Savior; so, made right by His grace, we could become heirs with the hope of eternal life. This sayin's trustworthy, and I want you to say it confidently, so believers make it their business to stay busy with good works. This stuff's good and useful for people;

web_text: but shun foolish questionings, genealogies, strife, and disputes about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain. Avoid a factious man after a first and second warning, knowing that such a one is perverted and sins, being self-condemned. When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, be diligent to come to me to Nicopolis, for I have determined to winter there.
slang_text: but steer clear of dumb arguments, family trees, fightin', and disputes over the law — that stuff's useless and empty. Avoid somebody stirrin' up cliques after givin' 'em one or two warnin's, knowin' a guy like that's twisted and sinnin', condemnin' himself with his own actions. When I send Artemas or Tychicus your way, hustle to come meet me in Nicopolis, 'cause I already decided to spend the winter there.

web_text: Send Zenas, the lawyer, and Apollos on their journey speedily, that nothing may be lacking for them. Let our people also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they may not be unfruitful. All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
slang_text: Send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos off on their trip quick, and make sure they got everything they need. Let our own folks learn to keep busy with good works for whatever's needed, so they don't end up unfruitful. Everybody here with me sends their greetin's. Greet everybody who loves us in the faith. Grace be with all of you. Amen.
